title: Perforated Sigmoid Diverticulitis
chief_complaint: Severe left lower abdominal pain for 4 days with fever and chills. I've had diverticulosis diagnosed on colonoscopy before. The pain is constant and now my whole belly hurts.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Diffuse peritonitis: Board-like rigidity, rebound tenderness throughout. Involuntary guarding. Absent bowel sounds. Maximal tenderness in LLQ.
physical_exam.Labs: WBC 22,500 with bandemia, Lactate 5.2, Creatinine 2.1 (AKI). CT: Perforated sigmoid diverticulitis with pneumoperitoneum (free air). Fecal peritonitis. Large pelvic abscess 8cm.

correct_diagnosis: Perforated Sigmoid Diverticulitis - Hinchey Stage IV (Feculent Peritonitis)
key_learning_points: Diverticulitis complications: Abscess (Hinchey I/II - contained), Purulent peritonitis (Hinchey III - perforation with pus), Feculent peritonitis (Hinchey IV - free perforation with stool). Hinchey IV is surgical emergency
Clinical: LLQ pain (sigmoid most common) with signs of peritonitis. CT with IV/PO/PR contrast is diagnostic: Shows diverticula, wall thickening, fat stranding, abscess, free air, or fistula
Treatment: Hinchey I/II: Antibiotics + Percutaneous drainage if abscess >3cm. Hinchey III: Surgical (laparoscopic lavage controversial - DILALA, LOLA trials). Hinchey IV: Emergency laparotomy

Surgery: Hartmann procedure (sigmoid resection + end colostomy + rectal stump) - standard for feculent peritonitis. Primary anastomosis with diverting loop ileostomy in select stable patients. Damage control surgery if unstable (temporary closure, planned re-operation)
Antibiotics: Piperacillin-Tazobactam or Meropenem (cover Gram-negatives + anaerobes). Add Vancomycin if MRSA risk. Post-op: NPO, NG decompression, IVF, ICU. Reversal of colostomy in 3-6 months
